source_intake_record.raw_question_text: Two identical unloaded generators are connected in parallel as shown in the <image>. Both generators have positive, negative, and zero sequence impedances of \( j0.4 \, \text{p.u.} \), \( j0.3 \, \text{p.u.} \), and \( j0.15 \, \text{p.u.} \), respectively. If the pre-fault voltage is \( 1 \, \text{p.u.} \), for a line-to-ground (L-G) fault at the terminals of the generators, the fault current, in p.u., is _______.
extraction_notes: problem field contains a leading answer-format hint that was not included in raw_question_text
image field is a PIL.Image object representation, not a path or URL

image_dependency: question explicitly says 'as shown in the image'
the circuit connection and fault location are diagram-based elements
multi_step: solving an L-G fault current requires combining positive, negative, and zero sequence networks
the two identical generators in parallel imply equivalent impedance reduction before fault calculation
the task is not direct lookup; it requires at least sequential network formation and numerical computation
verifiability: answer_type is numeric and answer_text is a single value
the problem provides explicit per-unit impedances and pre-fault voltage, enabling deterministic calculation
registry_passed=true and both question and answer are present

decision_reasons: question, image, and numeric answer are all present, so the core semantic assets are complete
registry passed with no reported issues or risk flags
high image dependency is adequately supported because the referenced image exists
multi-step and verifiability scores are high, indicating strong collection value and recoverability
